criteria.text: The applicant state must operate a State Office of Rural Health
criteria.source_quote: States with State Offices of Rural Health are eligible to apply.

criteria.text: Only one applicant from each state is eligible
criteria.source_quote: Only one applicant from each state is eligible, based on the designation of the governor.

criteria.text: The applicant must be designated by the governor of the state
criteria.source_quote: Only one applicant from each state is eligible, based on the designation of the governor.

criteria.text: The applicant must be a state government (or other eligible entity per additional eligibility information)
criteria.source_quote: State governments; Others (see text field entitled "Additional Information on Eligibility" for clarification)

criteria.text: The applicant must provide cost sharing
criteria.source_quote:
COST SHARING REQUIRED
Yes

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $223,410
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 223,410

criteria.text: Applicant must be a domestic organization (located in the 50 states, DC, Puerto Rico, Northern Mariana Islands, American Samoa, Guam, U.S. Virgin Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, Marshall Islands, or Palau).
criteria.source_quote: Only domestic organizations are eligible. "Domestic" means the 50 states, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Northern Mariana Islands, American Samoa, Guam, the U.S. Virgin Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Republic of Palau.

criteria.text: Applicant must be one of the listed eligible entity types (e.g., governments, school districts, businesses, institutions of higher education, nonprofits, tribal organizations).
criteria.source_quote: City or township governments; Independent school districts; County governments; Small businesses; State governments; Private institutions of higher education; For profit organizations other than small businesses; Native American tribal organizations (other than Federally recognized tribal governments); Public and State controlled institutions of higher education; Nonprofits having a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education; Special district governments; Native American tribal governments (Federally recognized); Nonprofits that do not have a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $600,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 600,000

criteria.text: Applicant must be an eligible entity as statutorily defined under 42 U.S.C. 290bb-7a.
criteria.source_quote: Eligible applicants are statutorily limited (per 42 U.S.C. 290bb-7a).

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $450,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 450,000

criteria.text: Individuals are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: Individuals and for-profit organizations are ineligible to apply for awards under this NOFO.

criteria.text: For-profit organizations are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: Individuals and for-profit organizations are ineligible to apply for awards under this NOFO.

criteria.text: Applicant must be a Qualified Youth Corps eligible to apply for projects developed under the Public Lands Corps Act.
criteria.source_quote: Therefore, eligible Youth Conservation Corps may only apply for projects developed under NOFO 15.243 – BLM Youth Conservation Opportunities on Public Lands.

criteria.text: Applicant must be one of the eligible entity types: state governments, nonprofits (with or without 501(c)(3) status), institutions of higher education, tribal governments/organizations, city/township/county/special district governments.
criteria.source_quote: State governments; Nonprofits that do not have a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education; Private institutions of higher education; Native American tribal governments (Federally recognized); City or township governments; Native American tribal organizations (other than Federally recognized tribal governments); County governments; Nonprofits having a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education; Special district governments; Public and State controlled institutions of higher education

criteria.text: Applicant must be able to provide cost sharing.
criteria.source_quote:
COST SHARING REQUIRED

Yes

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $220,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 220,000

criteria.text: Requested funding must be at least the award floor of $30,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award floor: 30,000

criteria.text: For single-function Research, Education, or Extension Projects, the applicant must be one of the eligible entity types: State Agricultural Experiment Station; college or university (including junior colleges offering associate degrees or higher); university research foundation; other research institution or organization; Federal agency; national laboratory; private organization or corporation; U.S. citizen, national, or permanent resident individual; or a group of two or more such entities.
criteria.source_quote: Eligible applicants for single-function Research, Education or Extension Projects include:a) State Agricultural Experiment Station;b) colleges and universities (including junior colleges offering associate degrees or higher);c) university research foundations;d) other research institutions and organizations;e) Federal agencies;f) national laboratories;g) private organizations or corporations;h) individuals who are U.S. citizens, nationals, or permanent residents; andi) any group consisting of two or more entities identified in a) through h).

criteria.text: The applicant institution must not be a foreign or international organization.
criteria.source_quote: Eligible institutions do not include foreign and international organizations.

criteria.text: For Integrated Projects, the applicant must be a college or university, a 1994 Land-Grant Institution, or a Hispanic-serving agricultural college or university.
criteria.source_quote: Eligible applicants for Integrated Projects include: a) colleges and universities;b) 1994 Land-Grant Institutions; andc) Hispanic-serving agricultural colleges and universities

criteria.text: For Integrated Projects under item a), a 'college' or 'university' must be an educational institution in a state that admits only secondary-education graduates, is legally authorized to provide postsecondary education, provides a program awarding a bachelor's or higher degree, is a public or other nonprofit institution, and is accredited by a nationally recognized accrediting agency.
criteria.source_quote: the terms “college” and “university” mean an educational institution in any state which: a) admits as regular students only persons having a certificate of graduation from a school providing secondary education, or the recognized equivalent of such a certificate; b) is legally authorized within such state to provide a program of education beyond secondary education; c) provides an educational program for which a bachelor’s degree or any other higher degree is awarded; d) is a public or other nonprofit institution; and e) is accredited by a nationally recognized accrediting agency or association.

criteria.text: The applicant must be able to provide cost sharing.
criteria.source_quote:
## COST SHARING REQUIRED
Yes

criteria.text: The requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $650,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 650,000

criteria.text: The requested funding must be at least the award floor of $10,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award floor: 10,000

criteria.text: The application must not be for dangerous gain-of-function research as defined in Section 8 of E.O. 14292.
criteria.source_quote: USDA will not accept competitive applications for grants and cooperative agreements submitted for due dates after June 20, 2025, and/or research and development (R&D) contract proposals submitted to solicitations issued after June 20, 2025, for dangerous gain-of-function research, as defined in Section 8 of E.O. 14292.

criteria.text: Applicant must apply for SNAP in the state where they currently live.
criteria.source_quote: To get SNAP benefits, you must apply in the state in which you currently live and you must meet certain requirements, including resource and income limits, which are described on this page.

criteria.text: Household must have no more than $3,000 in countable resources ($4,500 if at least one member is age 60 or older, or disabled).
criteria.source_quote: Currently, households may have $3,000 in countable resources (such as cash or money in a bank account) or $4,500 in countable resources if at least one member of the household is age 60 or older, or is disabled.

criteria.text: In most cases, household gross monthly income must be at or below the limit for its household size (130 percent of poverty).
criteria.source_quote:
Table 1: SNAP Income Eligibility Limits - Oct. 1, 2025, through Sept. 30, 2026 Household Size | Gross monthly income (130 percent of poverty) | Net monthly income (100 percent of poverty) | 
 1 | $1,696 | $1,305 | 
 2 | $2,292 | $1,763 | 
 3 | $2,888 | $2,221 | 
 4 | $3,483 | $2,680 | 
 5 | $4,079 | $3,138 |

criteria.text: In most cases, household net monthly income must be at or below the limit for its household size (100 percent of poverty).
criteria.source_quote: In most cases, your household must meet both the gross and net income limits described below or you are not eligible for SNAP and cannot receive benefits.

criteria.text: Applicant must meet SNAP work requirements (registering for work, not voluntarily quitting or reducing hours, taking a job if offered, and participating in employment and training programs if assigned).
criteria.source_quote: In general, people must meet work requirements to be eligible for SNAP.

criteria.text: Able-bodied adults without dependents must work or participate in a work program at least 20 hours per week to receive SNAP for more than 3 months in a 36-month period.
criteria.source_quote: able bodied adults without dependents are required to work or participate in a work program for at least 20 hours per week in order to receive SNAP benefits for more than 3 months in a 36-month period.

criteria.text: Students ages 18 through 49 enrolled in college at least half time are generally not eligible unless they meet specific exemptions.
criteria.source_quote: Generally, students ages 18 through 49 who are enrolled in college at least half time are not eligible for SNAP unless they meet certain specific exemptions .

criteria.text: Applicant must be a U.S. citizen or a certain lawfully present (eligible) non-citizen; undocumented non-citizens are not eligible.
criteria.source_quote: The Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 limits eligibility for SNAP benefits to U.S. citizens and certain lawfully present non-citizens.

criteria.text: Non-citizens must meet one of the qualifying criteria: lived in the U.S. at least 5 years, receiving disability-related assistance, or a child under 18.
criteria.source_quote:
Generally, to qualify for SNAP, non-citizens must meet one of the following criteria:
 Have lived in the United States for at least 5 years.
 Be receiving disability-related assistance or benefits.
 Be children under 18.

criteria.text: Applicant is generally not eligible if an institution provides most of their meals (with exceptions for elderly and disabled persons).
criteria.source_quote: Normally you are not eligible for SNAP benefits if an institution gives you most of your meals. There are exceptions for elderly persons and disabled persons .

criteria.text: Applicant must be a pregnant woman, a postpartum woman (up to 6 months after the end of pregnancy), a breastfeeding woman (up to the infant's first birthday), an infant, or a child up to their fifth birthday
criteria.source_quote:
Women who are currently pregnant, postpartum (up to 6 months after the ​end of a pregnancy​), or breastfeeding (up to the infant’s first birthday)
 Infants
 Children (up to their​ fifth birthday)

criteria.text: Applicant must apply through a WIC agency in their area (state, U.S. territory, or Tribe)
criteria.source_quote: You must apply through a WIC agency in your area. You might have several options for where and how to apply, including online.

criteria.text: Total household income must be at or below the WIC income limits for the applicant's household size, unless the applicant receives Medicaid, SNAP, or TANF
criteria.source_quote: If you don’t participate in one of these programs, your income must be at or below WIC limits.

criteria.text: Applicant must receive a free health check by WIC staff before enrollment
criteria.source_quote: All applicants must receive a free, simple health check by WIC staff before they are enrolled.

criteria.text: Applicant must have earned income.
criteria.source_quote: Have earned income

criteria.text: Applicant must have investment income below the annual limit.
criteria.source_quote: Have investment income below the limit

criteria.text: Applicant (and spouse if filing jointly, and any child claimed) must have a valid Social Security number issued on or before the due date of the return (including extensions).
criteria.source_quote: Have a valid Social Security number by the due date of your return (including extensions)

criteria.text: Applicant must be a U.S. citizen or a resident alien all year.
criteria.source_quote: Be a U.S. citizen or a resident alien all year

criteria.text: Applicant must not file Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income.
criteria.source_quote: Not file Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income

criteria.text: If separated from spouse and not filing a joint return, applicant must meet certain rules.
criteria.source_quote: Meet certain rules if you are separated from your spouse and not filing a joint tax return

criteria.text: The SSN must not have been issued solely to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit and must authorize work.
criteria.source_quote: It was issued solely to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit (such as Medicaid) and does not authorize you to work.

criteria.text: Applicant must have an eligible filing status: married filing jointly, head of household, qualifying surviving spouse, single, or married filing separate.
criteria.source_quote:
Eligible filing statuses to claim EITC:
 Married filing jointly
 Head of household 
 Qualifying surviving spouse 
 Single
 Married filing separate

criteria.text: A nonresident alien for any part of the year can only claim the EITC if filing status is married filing jointly, spouse is a U.S. citizen or resident alien, and the applicant chooses to be treated as a U.S. resident.
criteria.source_quote: If you were a nonresident alien for any part of the tax year, you can only claim the EITC if your filing status is married filing jointly and your spouse is a U.S. citizen or resident alien, and you choose to be treated as a U.S. resident.

criteria.text: When claiming the EITC without a qualifying child, applicant must have lived in the United States (50 states, DC, and U.S. military bases; not U.S. territories) for more than half the tax year.
criteria.source_quote: Lived in the United States for more than half the tax year. The United States includes the 50 states, the District of Columbia and U.S. military bases. It does not include U.S. territories such as Guam, the Virgin Islands or Puerto Rico

criteria.text: When claiming the EITC without a qualifying child, applicant cannot be claimed as a qualifying child or dependent of another person.
criteria.source_quote: Cannot be claimed as a qualifying child of another taxpayer or dependent of another person

criteria.text: When claiming the EITC without a qualifying child, applicant must be at least age 25 but under age 65 at the end of the year (if married filing jointly, at least one spouse must meet the age rule).
criteria.source_quote: Meet the age requirements . You must be at least age 25 but under age 65 at the end of the year. If married and filing a joint return, at least one spouse must meet the age rule.

criteria.text: Assistance is provided to households; LIHEAP does not provide direct grants to individuals.
criteria.source_quote: The Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) does not provide direct grants to individuals

criteria.text: The household must be low income to qualify for energy assistance.
criteria.source_quote: The Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) helps keep families safe and healthy through initiatives that assist families with energy costs.

criteria.text: Applicant (or someone in their household) participates in a qualifying government assistance program: Medicaid, SNAP, SSI, Federal Public Housing Assistance, Housing Choice Voucher, Project-based Rental Assistance, Public Housing, Affordable Housing Programs for American Indians/Alaska Natives/Native Hawaiians, or Veterans Pension and Survivors Benefit.
criteria.source_quote:
You can get Lifeline if you (or someone in your household) participate in one of these programs:
 Medicaid
 Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), formerly known as Food Stamps
 Supplemental Security Income (SSI)
 Federal Public Housing Assistance (FPHA)
 Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) Program (Section 8 Vouchers)
 Project-based Rental Assistance (PBRA)/2020/811
 Public Housing
 Affordable Housing Programs for American Indians, Alaska Natives, or Native Hawaiians
 Veterans Pension and Survivors Benefit

criteria.text: Applicants living on qualifying Tribal lands may qualify through participation in additional Tribal assistance programs (Bureau of Indian Affairs General Assistance, Tribal TANF, Head Start meeting income standard, Food Distribution Program on Indian Reservations).
criteria.source_quote:
If you live on qualifying Tribal lands , you can get Lifeline if you (or someone in your household) participate in:
 Any of the government assistance programs listed above
 Bureau of Indian Affairs General Assistance
 Tribal Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (Tribal TANF)
 Head Start (only households meeting the income qualifying standard)
 Food Distribution Program on Indian Reservations

criteria.text: Applicant's household income is at or below 135% of the 2026 Federal Poverty Guidelines, based on household size and state.
criteria.source_quote: You can get Lifeline if your income is at 135% or less than the 2026 Federal Poverty Guidelines. The guideline is based on your household size and state.

criteria.text: Applicant may qualify if their child or dependent participates in a qualifying program.
criteria.source_quote: You can also get Lifeline if your child or dependent participates in a qualifying program.

criteria.text: Survivors of domestic violence, human trafficking, or related crimes may participate if they provide proof of an attempted line separation request and are experiencing financial hardship.
criteria.source_quote: If you are a survivor, you can participate in the Lifeline program if you provide proof of an attempted line separation request and if you are experiencing financial hardship.

criteria.text: Survivors may qualify through extended eligibility criteria: household income at or below 200% of Federal Poverty Guidelines, enrollment in WIC, enrollment in Free and Reduced-Price School Lunch/Breakfast (including CEP schools), or receipt of a Federal Pell Grant in the current award year.
criteria.source_quote:
Survivors may qualify through existing Lifeline program requirements mentioned above or through the extended eligibility criteria:
 If your household income is at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines 
 Enrollment in the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
 Enrollment in the Free and Reduced-Price School Lunch or Breakfast program, including enrollment at a Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) school or school district
 Received a Federal Pell Grant in the current award year

criteria.text: Applicant must be a not-for-profit organization (including think tanks and civil society/non-governmental organizations) or a public or private educational institution.
criteria.source_quote: Not-for-profit organizations, including think tanks and civil society/non-governmental organizations. Public and private educational institutions.

criteria.text: Applicant must provide proof of legal registration in Zambia.
criteria.source_quote: Proof of legal registration and physical presence in Zambia

criteria.text: Applicant must have a physical presence in Zambia.
criteria.source_quote: Proof of legal registration and physical presence in Zambia

criteria.text: Applicant must submit an organizational chart showing key personnel.
criteria.source_quote: organizational chart showing key personnel

criteria.text: Applicant must submit letters of reference and past performance documentation.
criteria.source_quote: letters of reference and past performance documentation

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of 28,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 28,000

criteria.text: Requested funding must be at least the award floor of 10,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award floor: 10,000

criteria.text: Applicant must be one of the eligible organization types: U.S. or Dominican not-for-profit organizations, chambers of commerce and business associations, entrepreneurship and innovation hubs, technology and startup incubators/accelerators, higher education institutions, alumni associations, or civil society organizations with STEM/entrepreneurship/workforce development/youth programming experience.
criteria.source_quote:
● U.S. and Dominican not-for-profit organizations
● Chambers of commerce and business associations
● Entrepreneurship and innovation hubs
● Technology and startup incubators/accelerators
● Higher education institutions
● Alumni associations
● Civil society organizations with experience in STEM, entrepreneurship, workforce development, or youth programming

criteria.text: Applicant must be able to document non-profit status (U.S. organizations via 501(c)(3) IRS determination letter; non-U.S. organizations via government-issued evidence of non-profit status).
criteria.source_quote: Proof of Non-profit Status: Documentation to demonstrate the applicant’s non-profit status (e.g., U.S.-based organizations should submit a copy of their 501(c)(3) Internal Revenue Service determination letter, and non-U.S. organizations should provide evidence of non-profit status issued by a government entity).

criteria.text: Applicant must provide a copy of the organization's registration.
criteria.source_quote: Proof of Registration: A copy of the organization’s registration should be provided with the proposal application. U.S.-based organizations should submit a copy of their IRS determination letter.

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $80,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 80,000

criteria.text: Requested funding must be at least the award floor of $30,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award floor: 30,000

criteria.text: Cost sharing is not required for this opportunity.
criteria.source_quote:
COST SHARING REQUIRED
No

criteria.text: Applicant must be a State educational agency (SEA) or other public agency, including IDEA Part C lead agencies and State apprenticeship agencies.
criteria.source_quote: State educational agencies (SEAs) and other public agencies, including IDEA Part C lead agencies and State apprenticeship agencies.

criteria.text: To meet the absolute priority, the eligible applicant must be a State agency.
criteria.source_quote: To meet the absolute priority, eligible applicants must be a State agency, and if not the SEA or IDEA Part C lead agency, the applicant must partner with the SEA or IDEA Part C lead agency to implement the project.

criteria.text: If the applicant is not the SEA or IDEA Part C lead agency, it must partner with the SEA or IDEA Part C lead agency to implement the project.
criteria.source_quote: if not the SEA or IDEA Part C lead agency, the applicant must partner with the SEA or IDEA Part C lead agency to implement the project.

criteria.text: Local educational agencies (LEAs), institutions of higher education (IHEs), and public charter schools are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: other types of entities such as local educational agencies (LEAs), institutions of higher education (IHEs), and public charter schools, are not eligible to apply.

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $1,000,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 1,000,000

criteria.text: Applicant must be an eligible organization type (e.g., institution of higher education, government, nonprofit, for-profit, tribal organization, etc.).
criteria.source_quote: City or township governments; County governments; For profit organizations other than small businesses; Independent school districts; Native American tribal governments (Federally recognized); Native American tribal organizations (other than Federally recognized tribal governments); Nonprofits having a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education

criteria.text: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) entities (foreign organizations) are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) Entities (Foreign Organizations) are not eligible to apply.

criteria.text: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) components of U.S. organizations are not eligible, and foreign components are not allowed.
criteria.source_quote: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) components of U.S. Organizations are not eligible to apply.Foreign components, as defined in the NIH Grants Policy Statement, are not allowed.

criteria.text: Applicant must plan to start a tenure-track faculty position within a year of the award.
criteria.source_quote: targeted at applicants who plan to start a tenure-track faculty position within a year of the award

criteria.text: Independent clinical trials are not allowed under this award.
criteria.source_quote: NIA Career Transition Award (K22 Independent Clinical Trial Not Allowed)

criteria.text: Cost sharing is not required for this opportunity.
criteria.source_quote:
## COST SHARING REQUIRED
No

criteria.text: Applicant must be an eligible entity type (e.g., institutions of higher education, government entities, nonprofits, for-profit organizations, tribal governments/organizations, small businesses, housing authorities, or other listed types).
criteria.source_quote: City or township governments; County governments; For profit organizations other than small businesses; Independent school districts; Native American tribal governments (Federally recognized); Native American tribal organizations (other than Federally recognized tribal governments); Nonprofits having a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education; Nonprofits that do not have a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education

criteria.text: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) entities (foreign organizations) are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) Entities (Foreign Organizations) are not eligible to apply.

criteria.text: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) components of U.S. organizations are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) components of U.S. Organizations are not eligible to apply.

criteria.text: An accompanying Data Coordinating Center application under PAR-24-125 must be submitted simultaneously with the Clinical Coordinating Center application.
criteria.source_quote: Both a Clinical Coordinating Center application and a corresponding Data Coordinating Center (DCC) application need to be submitted simultaneously for consideration by NCCIH.

criteria.text: Cost sharing is not required for this opportunity.
criteria.source_quote:
## COST SHARING REQUIRED
No

criteria.text: Applicant must be an eligible entity type (e.g., city/township/county/state/special district governments, for-profit organizations other than small businesses, independent school districts, Native American tribal governments/organizations, nonprofits with or without 501(c)(3) status other than IHEs, private or public institutions of higher education, public/Indian housing authorities, or small businesses).
criteria.source_quote: City or township governments; County governments; For profit organizations other than small businesses; Independent school districts; Native American tribal governments (Federally recognized); Native American tribal organizations (other than Federally recognized tribal governments); Nonprofits having a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education; Nonprofits that do not have a 501(c)(3) status with the IRS, other than institutions of higher education

criteria.text: Project must support a new investigator in the early stages of establishing an independent career in tobacco regulatory research.
criteria.source_quote: This Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) aims to support new biomedical, behavioral, and social science investigators who are in the early stages of establishing independent careers in tobacco regulatory research.

criteria.text: Research project must address one or more High-Priority Research Topic(s) related to FDA CTP regulatory authority under the FSPTCA.
criteria.source_quote: Research projects must address one or more High-Priority Research Topic(s) related to the regulatory authority of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Center for Tobacco Products (CTP) as mandated by the Family Smoking Prevention and Tobacco Control Act (FSPTCA), Public Law 111-31.

criteria.text: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) entities (foreign organizations) are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) Entities (Foreign Organizations) are not eligible to apply.

criteria.text: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) components of U.S. organizations are not eligible to apply.
criteria.source_quote: Non-domestic (non-U.S.) components of U.S. Organizations are not eligible to apply.

criteria.text: Foreign components, as defined in the NIH Grants Policy Statement, are not allowed.
criteria.source_quote: Foreign components, as defined in the NIH Grants Policy Statement, are not allowed.

criteria.text: Requested funding must not exceed the award ceiling of $75,000.
criteria.source_quote: Award ceiling: 75,000

criteria.text: A corresponding CCC application must be submitted simultaneously with the DCC application for consideration by NCCIH.
criteria.source_quote: Both a DCC application and a corresponding CCC application need to be submitted simultaneously for consideration by NCCIH.

criteria.text: The trial must be relevant to the research mission of the NCCIH and considered a high priority by the Center.
